content:
- Read progress log, current_tasks.md, and current_tasks_done.md to enumerate active candidates.
- Found two active failing non-bug tasks: DCS simplification task 01 and logging simplification task 02.
- No active failing bugs in current_tasks.md, so selection is between non-bug failing tasks after checking blockers, priorities, and current codebase state.

- Read both candidate task files. Neither is a bug task, so bug-first rule is satisfied vacuously after confirming no active failing bugs.
- DCS task is a large architectural simplification with shared-type movement into src/state and broad downstream updates; logging task is also architectural but narrower to emission boundaries and best-effort semantics.
- Need direct priority tag check plus a quick repo-shape read before writing .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Checked candidate task files for explicit priority/blocker tags: neither has priority or blocked_by metadata.
- Compared architectural dependency direction against the current src tree.
- Chose the DCS simplification task as the next task because it is the more foundational boundary rewrite and the logging task currently references DCS worker paths likely to be removed or heavily reshaped by the DCS redesign, so doing logging first would create churn.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and found the active task: .ralph/tasks/story-dcs-simplification/01-task-rewrite-dcs-as-one-private-async-actor-with-one-public-opaque-view.md
- Ran progress_read on startup; there was no visible prior output, so I am treating this as a cold start unless later evidence says otherwise.
- Next I am reading the task file to determine whether it is still in TO BE VERIFIED or already in NOW EXECUTE.

- Read the full task file. It is an aggressive DCS redesign task with a lot of explicit design constraints and acceptance criteria.
- The next critical step is to find whether the task plan is still marked TO BE VERIFIED or has already moved to NOW EXECUTE, because the user wants different behavior depending on that marker.

- Inspected src/dcs/{mod,state,command,startup,store,worker,etcd_store,keys}. No code changes.
- Mapped public exports: mod.rs re-exports DcsCommand/DcsCommandError/DcsHandle plus the full DcsView family and DcsTrust. Current public DCS surface is much larger than the rewrite target.
- Confirmed production Arc/Mutex/threading live in src/dcs/etcd_store.rs: EtcdDcsStore owns Arc<AtomicBool>, Arc<Mutex<VecDeque<WatchEvent>>>, a dedicated OS thread, a tokio runtime inside that thread, std::sync::mpsc response channels, and a second keepalive thread/runtime for leader lease maintenance.
- Confirmed worker.rs itself is a single async loop over DcsWorkerCtx, but it is forced through Box<dyn DcsStore> and sync store methods, so the async ownership is split across modules.
- Confirmed redundant representation layers in state.rs: public *View types, crate-private *Record types, plus DcsCache. Also found dead/stale key support for /scope/config and /scope/init in keys.rs/store.rs/worker.rs; Config is decoded then ignored, InitLock is cached but not part of public DcsView.
- Confirmed startup.rs crate-private boundary is small and only runtime/node.rs appears to construct DCS, which supports collapsing most pub(crate) bootstrap types inward.
- Need next: final response should emphasize that the likely survivors are one public DcsView enum family, one crate-private DcsHandle, and minimal bootstrap/runtime wiring; DcsStore, EtcdDcsStore, WatchEvent/WatchOp, DcsCache, record types, key parser errors, DcsTrust, API advertisement, worker metadata, and the public DcsCommand enum are likely redesign casualties.

- Inspected the requested surfaces plus the adjacent DCS/PgInfo carrier types because the shared primitives cross those boundaries.
- Confirmed the main duplications are not only inside the requested folders: LeaseEpoch mirrors DcsLeaderView/LeaderLeaseRecord, SwitchoverTarget mirrors DcsSwitchoverTargetView/SwitchoverTargetRecord and API request parsing, WAL shape is split across state::WalLsn/TimelineId, dcs::WalVector, ha::WalPosition, and PgInfoState fields, and host/port is repeated in PgConnInfo, DCS member endpoints, Pg probe/process runtime structs, and start specs.
- Next I need to turn that into a concise file-by-file coordination map with exact file references and make clear which requested files are canonical consumers versus actual duplicate definitions.

- Finished the inspection-only pass and collected exact line references.
- The summary will emphasize that the requested folders are consumers of a wider duplication seam: DCS types mirror HA types for lease/switchover/WAL, and PgInfo/process mirror endpoint+conninfo concepts.
- No code changes were made. If this context window ended now, the next useful action would be proposing a concrete consolidation plan for one primitive at a time, starting with switchover target or endpoint types because they have the clearest translation boundaries.

- Added shared state ADTs under src/state: coordination.rs and net.rs, and re-exported them from src/state/mod.rs.
- Replaced src/dcs/command.rs with the target handle shape: crate-private DcsHandle, minimal DcsHandleError, and one-way typed switchover methods.
- Next I am rewriting the DCS public read model and the related startup/HA/API/process type definitions to depend on the new ADTs, without fixing compile errors yet.

- Rewrote the DCS public ADT in src/dcs/state.rs and switched startup advertisement typing to PgTcpTarget.
- Converted PgConnInfo toward a typed transport target and updated the local probe conversion to emit PgConnectTarget::Unix.
- While patching HA types I hit a stale context mismatch, so I am re-reading src/ha/types.rs before changing its coordination/switchover/epoch types.

- Finished the type-design phase for the DCS rewrite task and appended a concrete <plan> section ending in NOW EXECUTE to the task file.
- Changed the core ADTs first: new shared coordination/network primitives in src/state, new enum-based DcsView model in src/dcs/state.rs, crate-private one-way DcsHandle in src/dcs/command.rs, typed PgConnInfo transport target, and several downstream boundary signatures in startup/API/process/CLI/logging/HA.
- I intentionally did not fix compile errors or run make targets, because the task instructions for the no-marker branch require a type-first breaking pass and then an immediate stop once the design is marked NOW EXECUTE.
- Next turn should execute the plan as written: fix all compiler fallout, finish migrating downstream code, delete the old DCS bridge/store legacy, run make check/lint/test/test-long, then do docs with k2-docs-loop only after tests or goal completion.

- Started this turn by reading prior progress and the current Ralph task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-dcs-simplification/01-task-rewrite-dcs-as-one-private-async-actor-with-one-public-opaque-view.md.
- Next I am reading the task file to determine whether it is still in TO BE VERIFIED or already at NOW EXECUTE, because that gates whether I implement or stop.

- Read the task file and confirmed the plan already ends in NOW EXECUTE, so this turn must implement and validate rather than stop after design work.
- Next I am checking the worktree and reading the affected source tree so I can fix the current compile fallout without undoing the already-started ADT rewrite.

- Inspected git status: the initial ADT pass touched DCS/public consumer files and added src/state/{coordination,net}.rs, but old bridge files like src/dcs/store.rs and src/dcs/etcd_store.rs still remain.
- I am using make check as the compiler-driven inventory of remaining fallout, because the task is in NOW EXECUTE and the fastest safe path is to let the build point at every stale consumer and missing migration.

- Started an inspection-only turn for the DCS rewrite state.
- Read the prior progress log and confirmed the repo already has a partial ADT rewrite: new DcsView/DcsHandle shapes exist, but old DCS store/bridge internals still remain.
- Next I am reading only src/dcs/{mod.rs,command.rs,startup.rs,state.rs,worker.rs,store.rs,etcd_store.rs,keys.rs} plus directly required state modules so I can produce a concise implementation-oriented report with exact file references.

- Read the targeted DCS files and the shared state ADTs in src/state/{mod,coordination,net,ids,time}. The new public shape is real in src/dcs/state.rs and src/dcs/command.rs.
- Confirmed the main seam: src/dcs/state.rs now defines DcsView as NotTrusted/Degraded/Coordinated over ClusterView plus shared state::{LeaseEpoch, SwitchoverTarget, ObservedWalPosition, PgTcpTarget}, but src/dcs/worker.rs and src/dcs/store.rs still reference deleted legacy concepts like DcsTrust, DcsLeaderStateView, DcsMemberView, InitLockRecord, LeaderLeaseRecord, SwitchoverTargetRecord, DcsCommandRequest, and a control-plane store field that no longer exists.
- Ran make check. It fails with 93 errors. The first DCS-local compile blockers are exactly the stale internal references in src/dcs/store.rs and src/dcs/worker.rs, and then downstream consumers still importing the old DCS surface in cli/status.rs, ha/{decide,worker}.rs, process/worker.rs, logging/event.rs, and dev_support/api.rs.
- Next I am distilling that into a concise implementation report: what ADTs exist already, which DCS internals should be deleted or rewritten first, and the minimum compile path under the new DcsView/DcsHandle shape.

- Inspection-only pass for downstream DCS ADT fallout is underway; no files edited.
- Confirmed the new public surface in src/dcs/state.rs: DcsView is an enum with mode()/cluster()/observed_leadership()/is_coordinated(), ClusterView only exposes member_ids()/member_count()/member()/leadership()/switchover(), ClusterMemberView exposes postgres_target()/postgres(), and MemberPostgresView has readiness/system_identifier/timeline/is_primary/is_ready_replica/is_ready_non_primary/committed_wal/replay_wal/follow_wal/upstream helpers.
- The largest remaining fallout is in src/ha/worker.rs and src/cli/status.rs, which still expect old DcsTrust/DcsMemberView/DcsLeaderStateView/DcsSwitchoverStateView/raw field shapes. Process/postgres fallout is mostly PgConnInfo host/port assumptions that need to branch on PgConnectTarget.
- Runtime/startup wiring is mostly already aligned: runtime/node.rs hands DcsAdvertisedEndpoints::from_config into dcs::startup::bootstrap, and API/process startup layers just pass StateSubscriber<DcsView> through.
- I still need to turn the findings into the requested concise area-by-area report with concrete file references and likely helper methods to use, including tests/dev harness constructors like DcsView::empty and DcsHandle::closed that no longer fit the new API.

- Read the current progress log and inspected the six user-owned files.
- Confirmed the remaining breakage is localized to old DCS consumer assumptions in cli/process/dev-support plus PgConnectTarget rendering/tests.
- Next I am reading src/dcs/state.rs, API NodeState, postgres_managed_conf, and the deep process/status call sites so I can patch only the allowed files against the already-landed ADTs.

- Patched cli/status.rs to consume DcsView::cluster(), DcsMode, MemberPostgresView, and SwitchoverView instead of deleted DcsTrust/member-routing shapes.
- Patched process/worker.rs to resolve source members from the new cluster ADT as (member_id, ClusterMemberView), switched dev_support/api.rs and worker tests to DcsView::starting(), and updated postgres_managed.rs plus pginfo/conninfo.rs to the PgConnectTarget-based conninfo shape.
- Next I am doing a targeted residual-symbol search and then a focused cargo check over the owned-file fallout only; no cargo test.

- Replaced src/dcs/worker.rs with a direct async-actor implementation that owns etcd client/watch/leader-lease keepalive state, and added DcsEtcdConfig into the worker context.
- The next step is a focused compiler pass on DCS/startup/mod/logging so the new actor wiring is coherent before I retarget HA and the remaining consumers.

- Ran cargo check after the owned-file patch set. The crate is still red, but only due to broader non-owned DCS/HA/logging/runtime modules that still reference deleted legacy shapes.
- Fixed the two owned-file compile blockers that cargo surfaced in src/process/worker.rs by validating PgConnectTarget instead of the removed conninfo.host field.
- Final state for this turn: the six owned files no longer reference the deleted DcsTrust/DcsMemberView family or the old PgConnInfo host/port fields; remaining build failures are outside the allowed edit set.

- make check passes now; the remaining work before lint/tests is warning cleanup plus the required docs pass after all validation targets.
- I am removing dead DCS logging/event surface that survived from the old store bridge and gating test-only DCS constructors so clippy -D warnings can pass cleanly.

- Resumed the DCS simplification task in NOW EXECUTE mode.
- Verified the remaining blocker is lint.orphan_rust_files: the checker uses git ls-files, so deleted tracked Rust files still count until staged out of the index.
- Next I am staging only the src/dcs deletions/mods needed for the orphan-file check and rerunning make lint, then I will continue with make test and make test-long if lint goes green.

- make lint passed the orphan-rust-files gate after staging the src/dcs deletions.
- The remaining lint failure is a real clippy warning in src/cli/connect.rs: two useless format! calls around a static resolution error string.
- Next I am patching those two sites, rerunning make lint, and then proceeding to make test and make test-long.

- make lint now passes fully.
- The orphan-rust-files issue was resolved by staging the src/dcs deletions, and the only remaining code-side lint issue was two clippy useless-format warnings in src/cli/connect.rs.
- Next I am running the required test sequence: make test first, then make test-long, then docs via k2-docs-loop before final task bookkeeping.

- make test passed cleanly: 178 passed, 32 skipped.
- Next step is make test-long, which is the final required validation target before the docs update and task closure flow.

- make test-long exposed a real regression in the DCS rewrite.
- Failing scenario: ha_all_dcs_services_stopped_on_three_etcd_enters_safe_degraded_mode_and_fences_post_cutoff_writes. The step waiting for no operator-visible primary timed out because pgtm got a transport error for https://node-a:8443/state after DCS was stopped.
- Next I am inspecting the failing HA step, the state/API path, and the DCS/HA worker behavior under NotTrusted/Degraded transitions, then I will patch and rerun the focused long test before rerunning the full gate.

- Root cause found from preserved HA run artifacts: node containers exited because the rewritten DCS actor treated etcd watch-stream receive errors as fatal WorkerError values.
- In fail-safe scenarios after DCS quorum loss, compose logs showed `worker failed: dcs watch receive failed: grpc request error ... h2 protocol error`, which killed the whole node via runtime try_join and made /state unreachable.
- Patched src/dcs/worker.rs so watch receive errors flow through the existing DcsError reconnect path and republish NotTrusted instead of aborting the node. Next I am rebuilding and rerunning the previously failing long tests in isolation.

- Found the concrete failure source for the DCS quorum-loss scenarios by reading the latest HA artifact runs under tests/ha/runs.
- Node containers were exiting with `worker failed: dcs watch receive failed ... h2 protocol error` right after etcd loss; the rewritten DCS worker currently converts watch stream transport errors into fatal WorkerError instead of a recoverable DcsError reconnect path.
- The lagging-replica failure is a separate public-view bug: build_dcs_view exposes two Primary members at once when a stale old-primary member record survives after a new leader is elected.
- Next I am patching src/dcs/worker.rs to recover watch message errors and src/dcs/state.rs to canonicalize non-leader primary member views, then I will rerun make check/lint and the focused validations that I can reach from this environment.

- Fixed the node-exit regression by routing watch-stream receive failures through DCS reconnect instead of fatal WorkerError.
- Fixed the remaining fail-safe visibility regression by retaining the last observed cluster snapshot inside NotTrusted and exposing it through DcsView::cluster().
- Re-ran the previously failing ultra-long scenarios in isolation and they all pass now: ha_dcs_quorum_lost_fencing_blocks_post_cutoff_writes, ha_dcs_quorum_lost_enters_failsafe, ha_all_dcs_services_stopped_on_three_etcd_enters_safe_degraded_mode_and_fences_post_cutoff_writes, and ha_lagging_replica_is_not_promoted_during_failover.
- Next I am rerunning the full required validation gates from the final code state: make lint, make test, and a fresh full make test-long.

- Patched the DCS rewrite with two concrete fixes: watch stream transport errors are now treated as recoverable DCS disconnects in src/dcs/worker.rs, and src/dcs/state.rs now canonicalizes stale non-leader primary member records out of the public DcsView plus adds a regression unit test.
- make check and make lint pass after the patch.
- I verified the original ultra-long failures via tests/ha/runs artifacts: node processes were exiting because the DCS watch error was fatal, and the lagging-replica scenario showed two public primary slots at once under one leader epoch.
- Re-running make test from this tool context is blocked by environment-level Operation not permitted failures in port/dev-support/bind tests, and make test-long is blocked by docker socket permission denied from this execution context despite the user account being in the docker group.
- The task is not finishable from this tool context because the required make test/make test-long/docs/final bookkeeping gates cannot all be completed here. Next turn should either run in a context with working docker/socket/bind permissions or continue from the current patched code and artifact evidence.

- Full make test-long rerun is down to one remaining failure: ha_rewind_fails_then_basebackup_rejoins_old_primary.
- The failure is semantic, not transport: the old primary eventually returns but its DCS member role remains `unknown` instead of becoming `replica`, so the HA step waiting for replica behavior times out.
- Next I am inspecting the preserved HA artifacts for that run and the DCS state-building logic around stale/foreign primary records and replica publication after rewind/basebackup recovery.

- Resumed the active DCS simplification task and re-read the progress log plus current task pointer.
- Confirmed the task file is still the rewrite-DCS private async actor task and the worktree still contains the large in-flight rewrite plus the latest DCS/HA fixes.
- Next I am verifying the task document state, then I will finish the remaining validation gates from the latest code before docs/task closure.

- Confirmed the task file is still in NOW EXECUTE mode and <passes> remains false.
- The current closure path is to finish validation from the latest code: confirm or rerun make lint, then run a fresh full make test-long, then docs and final task bookkeeping.
- I am polling the earlier make lint session first to avoid redundant work before starting the long-test gate.

- make lint is still live but now clearly in the final strict clippy phase after finishing orphan-file, docs, and earlier clippy passes.
- Once lint exits cleanly I will run a fresh full make test-long from the latest code state; targeted long-test reruns already passed, but I still need one full gate for closure.

- make lint finished cleanly from the current code state.
- Started a fresh full make test-long run to get final runtime validation after the DCS/HA fixes; waiting on the HA suite now before docs and task closure.

- make test-long is confirmed actively running, not stalled: multiple HA ultra-long test binaries and compose fixtures are live in docker.
- Waiting for the full nextest summary before moving to docs and Ralph bookkeeping.

- The fresh full ultra-long run is still actively progressing through later HA cases, including the previously failing rewind/basebackup scenario.
- I am holding off on docs and task metadata until nextest produces the aggregate result from this final code state.

- The fresh full make test-long run failed at the end.
- Next I am reading the nextest JUnit and exported logs to identify the exact failing ultra-long scenario and inspect whether it is a new regression or a full-suite interaction.

- Found the fresh full-suite failure mode for ha_primary_killed_then_rejoins_as_replica: node-b was looping successful pg_basebackup jobs without ever starting postgres.
- Root cause: HA observation still reused stale self timeline/system_identifier from DCS after a successful basebackup, so the node kept looking mismatched and re-requesting basebackup.
- Patched src/ha/worker.rs to ignore stale DCS self identity while a basebackup success is awaiting restart, and added a regression test for that classification.
- Next I am re-running make check and then the failing long scenario before another full make test-long.

- make check passed after the HA observation fix.
- The previously failing focused ultra-long scenario ha_primary_killed_then_rejoins_as_replica now passes, confirming node-b no longer loops basebackup forever.
- I am re-running the remaining fast gates from the latest code state (make lint and make test) before a final fresh full make test-long.

- Latest code state is now green on make check, make lint, and make test after the HA observation fix.
- The previously failing focused long scenario also passes.
- Started a fresh full make test-long run from this revalidated state; docs and Ralph closure remain blocked on this final suite.

- The fresh full make test-long rerun is actively progressing through later failover and partition scenarios after the fix; no new failure signal yet.
- Still waiting on the aggregate nextest result before docs and final task closure.

- All required validation gates are now green from the latest code state: make check, make lint, make test, and a fresh full make test-long.
- Switching to the required docs-update phase now via the k2-docs-loop skill before final task closure.

- Integrated the new trust-model explanation page into docs/src/explanation and added it to docs/src/SUMMARY.md.
- Running docs validation now (make docs-lint and make docs-build) to verify the new mermaid block and mdBook integration.
